exemplar.title: Analyzing Geometric Efficiency in Perfume Bottle Design: A Comparative Study of Volume and Surface Area

detailedExemplarData.title: Analyzing Geometric Efficiency in Perfume Bottle Design: A Comparative Study of Volume and Surface Area

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequence from introduction through conclusion
Consistent use of headings, tables and figures to guide the reader
weaknesses: Repetition of standard definitions and overly long algebra tables
Cluttered presentation of polynomial expansions and large tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the exploration is coherent and logically developed (band 3), significant digressions and unnecessary repetitions reduce its conciseness and overall clarity. The work is organized but not exceptionally clear, placing it in band 2 for Presentation.

strengths: Correct use of integrals, limits, derivatives and function notation most of the time
Effective combination of equations, tables and 2D/3D graphs to crossâvalidate results
Logical explanations precede calculations and guide the reader through each step
weaknesses: Lowâresolution and cropped images hamper interpretation
Inconsistent domain notation and occasional omission of the differential âdxâ
Some long algebra dumps disrupt clarity and conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language, multiple representations and logical clarity are all solidly in band 3. Minor notation inconsistencies and figure quality issues prevent this from reaching band 4, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Use of personal context (own bottles, photographs) shows genuine ownership of the topic
Application of trapezoidal estimation for lateral surface area demonstrates creativity
Physical measurement and bespoke modelling choices reflect independent thinking
weaknesses: Mathematical methods largely follow standard textbook approaches
Limited exploration of alternative mathematical pathways or challenging of assumptions
Approach is personal but not highly innovative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work clearly reflects the studentâs personal interest and shows creativity, placing it in band 2. However, it does not demonstrate the level of innovation or depth of independent thinking required for band 3.

strengths: Reflection on measurement error and modelling assumptions is present
Comparison of surfaceâarea-to-volume ratios linked to sustainability and cost
Evaluation of outcomes includes clear implications and mentions of extensions
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive with limited critical depth
No discussion of interpolation error or error propagation
Lacks deep mathematical critique or exploration of alternative models
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of results place the work in band 2. The absence of deeper critical reflection and comprehensive evaluation prevents a higher band, justifying a score of 2.

strengths: Comprehensive use of piecewise Lagrange interpolation and definite integrals
Solid justification for subtracting overlapping volumes
Calculus applied across multiple non-trivial shapes with generally correct set-ups
weaknesses: Surface-area formula misapplied (missing x-factor) in some integrals
No real-world data check or validation of numerical results
High-degree polynomials introduce potential numerical instability without comment
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration displays a thorough understanding of calculus and interpolation at SL level, meriting band 4. Minor conceptual slips in surface-area calculations and the lack of a real-world validation check prevent placement in bands 5 or 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations typically precede calculations, the logic of subtracting overlapping volumes is spelt out, and the reader can follow why each integral limit is chosen.  A few long algebra dumps disrupt clarity, so the descriptor âexcellentâ is not fully met.

For each bottle, the student presents correct integral set-ups and justifies domain choices.  Numeric answers are sensible and internally consistent.  Nevertheless, the surface-area expression omits the âxâ factor present in the standard formula for revolution about the x-axis and there is no check of reasonableness against real-world bottle volumes, showing that accuracy is âgoodâ but not âcompletely correctâ.

The student supplies equations, point tables, 2-D graphs, 3-D Desmos renderings and numerical tables.  These representations complement each other and help verify results; however some images are low-resolution or partially cropped, so the use is effective rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration follows a clear sequence: introduction â methodology â modelling â volume â surface area â ratio â conclusion.  Each part builds on the previous one and the reader can follow the mathematical story without having to infer missing steps.  Minor logical breaks (e.g. sudden insertion of the Economics digression and occasional repetition of formula explanations) prevent the work from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall coherence is strong.

Applying Lagrange interpolation to physical outlines and extending surface-area formulae to non-textbook shapes displays creativity and initiative beyond classroom routine.  Yet the innovation is confined to adapting known tools rather than creating new mathematical techniques, placing the work solidly in band 2.

Results are compared via SA:V ratios and linked back to cost and sustainability.  The student comments on strengths and limitations of the method, showing thoughtful analysis, but there is limited critical examination of mathematical accuracy or alternative models, so the second band descriptor fits best.

The conclusion identifies Jean Paul Gaultier as optimal and briefly discusses implications for marketing and environment.  Possible extensions are mentioned.  Evaluation of outcomes is clear but not comprehensiveâthere is no quantitative check (e.g. comparing to manufacturing data) or future mathematical pathwayâso band 2 is appropriate.

The student reflects on measurement error, modelling assumptions (volume of revolution, excluding lids) and acknowledges the ecological motivation.  Reflection appears in several sections, but tends to remain descriptive; it does not probe deeper mathematical implications, hence âmeaningfulâ rather than âsubstantialâ.

Integrals, limits, derivatives and function notation are almost always used correctly and symbols are typeset clearly.  Small slips such as missing differential âdxâ in a few places and occasional misuse of subscripts prevent the work from being âconsistently accurate throughoutâ.

The work unmistakably reflects the studentâs personal interests (perfume design, sustainability) and choices (own bottles, own photographs).  The approach is personal but not particularly innovativeâmethods follow standard techniques rather than novel constructionsâthus the middle band is most fitting.

The student often repeats standard definitions (e.g. Riemann sum, Lagrange interpolation) at length and includes lengthy polynomial expansions that add little to the readerâs understanding.  There is also an extended, only tangentially related Economics discussion.  Most material is relevant, but the overall piece is not concise.

Calculus of volumes and surface areas, piecewise Lagrange interpolation and manipulation of high-degree polynomials constitute mathematics that is well beyond the basics required at SL, used across three non-trivial shapes.  The mathematics is comprehensive and central to the exploration; minor conceptual slips stop it short of âexpertâ (band 6).

Choosing a personal context, taking physical measurements, and deciding to stitch multiple Lagrange polynomials show clear independent thinking.  However, most of the mathematics applied is standard textbook calculus; there is little evidence of exploring alternative mathematical pathways or challenging assumptions, so the highest band is not justified.

Headings, numbered tables, and figures are used consistently; the reader is always told where a value is coming from.  Introduction, body and conclusion are all present.  Some figure captions are truncated or mis-labelled and there is a degree of clutter in long tables of algebra, so the organisation is âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionally well organisedâ.

All principal toolsâpolynomial interpolation, definite integrals, surface-area integralsâare HL-level and are applied to unfamiliar contexts.  Breadth and depth match HL expectations and are integrated effectively; a few misapplied surface-area formulae prevent the award of the top band.

annotations.comment: The title is descriptive but could more explicitly frame the mathematical focus and research question to orient the reader.
The extended discussion of IB Economics interrupts the mathematical narrative; consider trimming this digression to maintain focus and coherence.
There is no discussion of interpolation error or goodness of fit; include a brief reflection on potential inaccuracies from high-degree polynomials.
The 3D render is cropped by the margins, making it hard to see the full shape; adjust the layout or provide a separate full-page figure with proper captions.
The figure lacks axis scales and unit labels; include clearly marked axes to verify that the plotted shape matches real measurements.
The continuous table of polynomial segments is overwhelming; break this into multiple tables or present only final simplified expressions.
Domain notation varies between braces and parentheses; adopt a consistent format such as $$\{x\mid a\le x\le b\}$$ across all segments.
Subtracting overlapping volumes is well justified and shows solid understanding of the volumeâofârevolution concept.
State the research question or aim explicitly in the introduction rather than only naming the IB internal assessment level.
The bottle images are low-resolution and partially cropped, which hampers interpretation; replace with higher-quality figures and clear annotations.
The integral notation omits the differential âdxâ; ensure the formula reads $$V=\pi\int_a^b y^2\,dx$$ to demonstrate correct mathematical conventions.
The general Lagrange interpolation formula is presented fully but would be clearer if streamlined to the specific degree used, with notation simplified.
Uncertainty bounds are given in the table but no error propagation is performed; include a brief analysis of how measurement uncertainty affects final results.
Show a sample substitution into $$r=\tfrac{\text{circumference}}{2\pi}$$ for one bottle to clarify how the radius values were obtained.
The large coordinate table is difficult to follow; consider splitting it into smaller tables or summarizing the key points to improve organization.
Arrows and unit labels are used inconsistently in coordinate lists; standardize notation and include units (cm) for each coordinate entry.
The traced outline image is pixelated and axis labels are unclear; use a higher-resolution graphic and explicitly label the axes to aid interpretation.
Add a brief transition explaining how the methodology leads into the Lagrange modeling to strengthen the logical flow.
The explicit Lagrange expressions are correct but overly long; introduce a summation notation to make the presentation more concise.
The volume calculation omits details on how the integrals were evaluated; specify the numerical method or software settings and discuss estimated error.
The list of functions and domains is comprehensive, but restating the reasoning for each boundary choice would improve clarity.
Subtracting the blue segment is correct, but a shaded sketch indicating the removed volume would strengthen the explanation.
The high-degree polynomial for the final segment may introduce numerical instability; include a note on how coefficient magnitudes were validated.
The cumulative integral result is correct but would benefit from a table showing each segmentâs individual volume to increase transparency.
The innovative use of trapezoids to estimate lateral surface area demonstrates strong personal engagement and creativity.
The assumption $$f(x_A)\approx f(x_B)$$ simplifies the integral, but discuss the error this introduces and its impact on the surfaceâarea estimate.
